narration: Day one. I'm out at the lake cabin until spring. No road, no neighbors, just me. The place is older than the map said, but the quiet is kind of perfect. That hum started at sunset. I thought it was the generator. It isn't. It's coming from outside, from the lake. There are three lights out there, low over the ice. They don't blink, they don't move, they just sit there. Planes don't do that. And then the radio, there's no one on this channel, the radio said two words. Stay inside. I didn't sleep. This morning there are tracks in the snow, all the way around the cabin. They aren't mine. And they aren't human.

shots.prompt: Silent POV phone footage, handheld, moving through a dim wooden cabin lit only by a lantern and the phone-screen glow. The camera pans across a wood stove, a sleeping bag and an old CB radio on a table. Cozy but isolated. No people on screen. Soft film grain.

Silent POV phone footage slowly panning the frosted cabin window from inside; outside, deep untouched snow and a dark wall of pines under cold blue moonlight. The old radio on the table crackles faintly with static. Quiet, still, eerie. No people.

Silent POV phone footage stepping outside onto deep snow at night, a headlamp beam cutting the dark, breath fog drifting past the lens. The camera finds a dark treeline and, far across a frozen lake, three faint lights pulsing low over the ice. Cold blue moonlight, handheld sway. No people visible.

Silent shaky phone footage digital-zooming on three faint glowing lights hovering low over the frozen lake, pulsing slowly in unison. The autofocus hunts and the dark image grain crawls. Mysterious and suspenseful. No people on screen.

Silent POV phone footage inside the dark cabin, close on the old CB radio as its dial glows and the needle twitches with bursts of static and a faint rhythmic pulse. A single gloved hand reaches into frame to touch the dial. Tense, intimate, low light. No face shown.

Silent POV phone footage at cold first light, looking down at fresh tracks pressed into deep snow, evenly spaced and clearly not human, circling the cabin and leading off toward the dark pines. Breath fog shakes the frame. The shot ends on the tracks vanishing into the treeline. No people.
